diagram_title: Managed MCP App Market + Google Calendar App — C4 Component Diagram

systems.name: Opencode Runtime
systems.description: Canonical multi-interface runtime hosting managed apps and tool execution.

systems.name: Google Calendar API
systems.description: External calendar service consumed by the managed Google Calendar app.

containers.name: Backend Runtime
containers.description: Server-side runtime, tool, auth, and app lifecycle container.

containers.name: Web/TUI Management Surface
containers.description: Operator-facing Web and TUI surface for catalog visibility, install/config/auth actions, and lifecycle observability.

components.name: App Registry Service
components.description: Single backend authority for built-in managed app catalog, install states, lifecycle transitions, and runtime ownership.

components.name: Canonical Auth Service
components.description: Owns OAuth/account identity binding for managed apps.

components.name: Google Calendar App Adapter
components.description: Managed app adapter exposing Google Calendar config contract, tool surface, and fail-fast runtime boundary.

components.name: LLM Tool Router
components.description: Routes natural-language intent into managed app tool execution with no silent fallback to alternate accounts/providers/apps.

components.name: App Management UI
components.description: Displays authoritative app catalog, install/config/auth/runtime state, and remediation cues for Web/TUI operators.

relationships.description: reads authoritative app catalog and sends install/configure/enable actions
requests OAuth/account binding for app capabilities under canonical ownership
activates and configures managed Google Calendar app adapter without external MCP server wiring
invokes app tool surface for calendar operations and receives explicit fail-fast errors
depends on canonical auth/account binding before ready state
